statement: In 2024, Iceland's goods exports were dominated by three sectors: marine products (~39% of total goods exports), aluminium and other metals (~33%), and manufactured goods including pharmaceuticals and medical devices (~12%). Services exports, led by tourism, have grown to roughly equal goods exports in value.
caveats: Export composition is volatile. Aluminium exports depend on global commodity prices and smelter output. Marine product share has declined from over 70% in the 1990s as the economy has diversified.

related_entries.statement: The European Union is Iceland's largest trading partner. In 2024, approximately 50% of Iceland's goods exports and 60% of goods imports by value were with EU member states. Marine products (primarily fish) constitute the largest single export category to the EU.

Outside the EU, Iceland's largest goods trade partners are the United Kingdom (~12% of goods exports in 2024), Norway (~5%), the United States (~8%), and China (~4%). The UK's share increased after Brexit as UK trade was reclassified from EU to non-EU, but the underlying trade volume remained broadly stable.
